title: Increase pidWaitTimeout to 60s
body:
At scale, it appears that we sometimes hit the 1000ms timeout to create
the PID file when a container is created or executed.
Increasing the value to 60s should help when running a lot of containers
in heavy-loaded environment.

Related #1495
Fixes #1816
Signed-off-by: Emilien Macchi <emilien@redhat.com>

comments.body: This looks a bit like a whack a mole. Can't we turn it up to something really large (e.g., 60 seconds) where we can be sure the system is in a "broken" state? In the end, we just want to make sure that we terminate at some point, do we?

Pull-request updated, HEAD is now c3d8328150d12bd0981a43aeea2924bcc4d5c1cc
With 60s of timeout.
